key_insight: MADA-RL turns a compact math model into coordinated generator and critic agents and uses a counterfactual debate-aware reward to improve reasoning with only a small LoRA-tuned parameter budget.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a parameter-efficient post-training recipe for deploying stronger reasoning in compact models, trading a modest accuracy gain for far lower training cost and trainable-parameter count.
method: Specializes the model into generator and critic roles.
Trains only LoRA adapter parameters with a debate-aware signal.
Uses a counterfactual critic advantage that benchmarks critic reward against generator ensemble accuracy.
evidence_signal: On five mathematical reasoning benchmarks, DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.5B improved from 39.9% to 41.9% (+2.0 points, p < 0.001) with 16× fewer trainable parameters than fully fine-tuned baselines.
watch_item: The gains are modest and still fall short of the strongest larger-data baselines, so inference-time multi-round debate cost and data scale remain practical concerns.
paper_title: MADA-RL: Multi-Agent Debate-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Parameter-Efficient Reasoning in Compact Models
